subtitle.description: — A protocol for seeing how your judgment changes after talking with AI.

hero.coreQuestion: "After interacting with this AI, where did the human judgment move?"

keyPrinciple.label: Key Principle:
keyPrinciple.text: JDVP takes a clear position on <measures>making delegation visible</measures>. It does not <evaluates>judge delegation itself as good or bad</evaluates>.

concept.title: What JDVP Does
concept.description: JDVP shows whether judgment stayed with the human, moved toward AI, or became shared during an interaction.

observer.title: It observes, not scores
observer.description: JDVP records how judgment changes during a conversation without grading that change.

measure.title: It tracks direction, not a score
measure.description: The point is to see where judgment moved, not to collapse everything into one rating.

buffer.title: It is not an anti-AI brake
buffer.description: The goal is not to block use, but to make the shift in judgment easier to notice.

temporal.title: It cares about the flow
temporal.description: One moment matters less than the pattern from the start of the conversation to the end.

constraints.title: What JDVP Does Not Do
constraints.noScoring: It does not rank AI systems
constraints.noNormative: It does not judge delegation as good or bad
constraints.noRecommendations: It does not tell users the one right move
constraints.noAggregation: It does not turn measurements into authority scores
